tags.name: Users
tags.description: Look up and manage professional-network user profiles — retrieve a profile, list connections and followers, check InMail credit balance, and endorse a skill.

tags.name: Companies
tags.description: Look up company profiles, browse employee lists, and retrieve company posts, jobs, and followers.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Search for people, companies, posts, and jobs across the professional network.

tags.name: Messaging
tags.description: Send messages, manage conversations, and track message threads with connections.

tags.name: Comments
tags.description: Comment on posts and manage comments — comment on a post, reply to a comment, update or delete a comment, list a user's comments and a comment's replies, and list, add, or remove comment reactions.

tags.name: Invites
tags.description: Send, list, withdraw, accept, and decline connect-requests on the professional network.

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Retrieve a public job posting's full detail by id.

tags.name: Posts
tags.description: Read, publish, and delete posts, and react to them — get a post, list a user's posts (your own or another's), create or delete a post, list and add reactions, list a user's reactions, and remove a reaction. Commenting lives in the Comments group.

tags.name: Recruiter: User Profile
tags.description: Look up a Recruiter-enriched member profile.

tags.name: Recruiter: Messaging
tags.description: Start Recruiter InMail conversations.

tags.name: Recruiter: Search
tags.description: Resolve filter parameters and search people, the talent pool, or a pasted search URL.

tags.name: Recruiter: Projects
tags.description: Manage hiring projects and their candidate pipeline.

tags.name: Recruiter: Jobs
tags.description: Manage the full job-posting lifecycle and review talent-pool applicants.

tags.name: Sales Navigator: Messaging
tags.description: Start Sales Navigator InMail conversations.

tags.name: Sales Navigator: User Profile
tags.description: Look up a Sales Navigator-enriched member profile.

tags.name: Sales Navigator: Lists
tags.description: Manage saved account and lead lists — list, browse, and save into them.

tags.name: Sales Navigator: Search
tags.description: Resolve filter parameters and search people, companies, or a pasted search URL.

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: Manage connected professional network accounts — list, inspect, update, and disconnect.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Connect and re-authenticate professional network accounts — hosted links, direct credential/cookie sign-in, verification checkpoints, and connect-session status.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Register and manage webhooks that deliver real-time account events.

Error.description: Structured error envelope. Every non-2xx response uses this shape.

message.description: Human-readable explanation. Safe to surface to an end user.

retry_hint.description: How to retry, when retrying may help.

delay_ms.description: Suggested wait before retry, in milliseconds.

user_fixable.description: True when the caller can resolve this by changing the request.

retry_likely_to_succeed.description: True when an identical retry is likely to succeed.

required_tier.description: Present on tier-gated denials — the seat tier required to proceed.

example.message: The referenced resource does not exist for this tenant.

RateLimit-Policy.description: Quota policy for the current window. Structured-field format: "tenant";q=<quota>;w=<window_seconds>. q is the total requests allowed; w is the window length in seconds.

RateLimit.description: Current usage within the window. Structured-field format: "tenant";r=<remaining>;t=<seconds_until_reset>. r is the remaining request budget; t is seconds until the window resets.

Retry-After.description: Seconds to wait before retrying. Present on 429 responses only.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: The account ID (`acc_…`) to use for the request.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: "me" for the caller's own account, or another LinkedIn user's public identifier, member ID, or profile URL. Prefer the public identifier (vanity handle): some profile sections return a richer view for handle lookups — notably the skills section's `endorsement_id`, which may be omitted on member-ID lookups.

parameters.name: linkedin_sections
parameters.description: Which profile sections to fetch (repeatable, flat). Pass one or more of: linkedin_experience, linkedin_education, linkedin_languages, linkedin_skills, linkedin_certifications, linkedin_volunteer_experience, linkedin_projects, linkedin_recommendations, linkedin_interests (or linkedin_* for all), plus each value's _preview variant. Omit for base fields only.

200.description: The requested user's profile (classic variant). Section arrays are present only when requested via linkedin_sections.

schema.description: A LinkedIn user's profile (classic variant). Section arrays under specifics (skills, experience, education, etc.) are only present when the corresponding linkedin_sections value was requested. No inline quota — read quota from GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}.

public_identifier.description: LinkedIn public URL slug (e.g. 'jane-doe').

profile_url.description: Full LinkedIn profile URL.

private_picture_download_url.description: A private, time-limited picture download URL (present only in some responses).

description.description: Profile summary/about section. Passes through verbatim — never stored.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when this profile record was created on the platform.

emails.description: Email addresses associated with the profile.

phone_numbers.description: Phone numbers associated with the profile.

is_blocked.description: Whether the authenticated account has blocked this member.

is_following.description: Whether the authenticated account follows this member.

specifics.description: Structured profile-lens data, incl. connection distance and (when requested) section arrays.

network_distance.description: Connection distance from the operator account.

can_send_inmail.description: Whether an InMail can be sent to this member.

pronoun.description: The member's stated pronoun, when set.

notify_visit_token.description: An opaque token observed on the wire; no documented consumer yet.

is_open_profile.description: Whether the member has Open Profile enabled.

is_premium.description: Whether the member has a LinkedIn Premium subscription.

is_influencer.description: Whether the member is a LinkedIn influencer.

is_creator.description: Whether the member is a LinkedIn creator.

is_hiring.description: Whether the member has hiring mode active.

is_open_to_work.description: Whether the member has Open to Work enabled.

experience.description: Work experience entries. Present only when requested via linkedin_sections.

education.description: Education entries. Present only when requested via linkedin_sections.

post.description: Endorses one specific skill on a target member's profile. To obtain the endorsement_id: (1) GET /v1/{account_id}/users/{user_id}?linkedin_sections=linkedin_skills, (2) read the target skill's endorsement_id from the skills section, (3) call this endpoint with that value. user_id must be the LinkedIn member ID (ACo… format) — a public identifier does not resolve on this endpoint.

linkedin_only.description: Present and true when the event carries LinkedIn-specific content.

availability.description: Delivery timing. Omitted (equivalent to 'realtime') on every live-pushed event. 'no_longer_realtime' means no push exists for this event; 'not_realtime' means the event is polled, not pushed, and may lag the underlying LinkedIn event.

events.description: An inbound message arrived in any chat (classic / InMail).
An outbound message was delivered.
A message in a chat was read by the recipient.
A reaction was added to a message.
A message was edited after send.
A message was deleted.
A chat's container state changed (e.g. archived, muted, read-state) — useful for inbox automation.
A chat thread was deleted.
A pending invitation was accepted by the recipient (new relation). Polled — up to ~8h latency.

A new relation appeared on the account (any new LinkedIn connection, not just ones you invited). Polled, not real-time — up to ~4h delay; a freshly connected account may see a backfill burst of these on first connect.
A new account link completed successfully for the first time.
The account health check reported a healthy, connected state.
A synchronization cycle completed successfully. No longer pushed in real time — v2 has no equivalent event; no read-path alternative exists.
An existing account was re-authorized in place.
Credentials expired — the account needs reconnection.
The initial account-link attempt failed terminally. No longer pushed in real time — detect via an on-demand account read.
The account was removed from the platform (terminal).
Account synchronization encountered an error (not a credentials issue — needs investigation).
Account synchronization was stopped or paused externally. Not an error — may resume automatically.
An account link is in progress. Transient and high-frequency — opt in explicitly. No longer pushed in real time — v2 has no equivalent event.
A LinkedIn-side scope the account relies on was withdrawn. No longer pushed in real time — detect via an on-demand account read.
The initial history backfill for a newly connected account has started (informational — bracket-open).
The initial history backfill completed — full LinkedIn history is now queryable.
The initial history backfill failed. The delivered payload carries a neutral `reason` — one of `internal`, `proxy`, or `provider`.
A candidate was added to a hiring project pipeline.
A candidate pipeline status changed.
An InMail arrived on a Sales Navigator seat.

patch.description: Update a webhook in place. Every field is optional — a provided field replaces its value, an omitted field is left unchanged. The source is immutable and the signing secret is preserved (never re-issued).

events.description: Replace the subscribed events (whole-array). Every event must be a canonical event whose source matches the webhook's source.

request_url.description: Replace the delivery URL. Must be an HTTPS URL.

name.description: Replace the human-readable name (1–100 chars), or null to clear it.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the webhook. A disabled webhook delivers nothing.

headers.description: Replace the custom headers added to each delivery POST (whole-array).

data.description: Replace the field-remapping keys for the delivery payload (whole-array).

account_ids.description: Replace the targeted accounts (whole-array). Must be non-empty — each id must be an acc_-prefixed id owned by the tenant.

disable.summary: Disable a webhook (delivers nothing until re-enabled)

changeUrlAndEvents.summary: Replace the delivery URL and the subscribed events
